    21	
    22	"""Merge driver support for dulwich."""
    23	
    24	__all__ = [
    25	    "MergeDriver",
    26	    "MergeDriverRegistry",
    27	    "ProcessMergeDriver",
    28	    "get_merge_driver_registry",
    29	]
    30	
    31	import os
    32	import subprocess
    33	import tempfile
    34	from collections.abc import Callable
    35	from typing import Protocol
    36	
    37	from .config import Config
    38	
    39	
    40	class MergeDriver(Protocol):
    41	    """Protocol for merge drivers."""
    42	
    43	    def merge(
    44	        self,
    45	        ancestor: bytes,
    46	        ours: bytes,
    47	        theirs: bytes,
    48	        path: str | None = None,
    49	        marker_size: int = 7,
    50	    ) -> tuple[bytes, bool]:
    51	        """Perform a three-way merge.
    52	
    53	        Args:
    54	            ancestor: Content of the common ancestor version
    55	            ours: Content of our version
    56	            theirs: Content of their version
    57	            path: Optional path of the file being merged
    58	            marker_size: Size of conflict markers (default 7)
    59	
    60	        Returns:
    61	            Tuple of (merged content, success flag)
    62	            If success is False, the content may contain conflict markers
    63	        """
    64	        ...
    65	
    66	
    67	class ProcessMergeDriver:
    68	    """Merge driver that runs an external process."""
    69	
    70	    def __init__(self, command: str, name: str = "custom"):
    71	        """Initialize process merge driver.
    72	
    73	        Args:
    74	            command: Command to run for merging
    75	            name: Name of the merge driver
    76	        """
    77	        self.command = command
    78	        self.name = name
    79	
    80	    def merge(
    81	        self,
    82	        ancestor: bytes,
    83	        ours: bytes,
    84	        theirs: bytes,
    85	        path: str | None = None,
    86	        marker_size: int = 7,
    87	    ) -> tuple[bytes, bool]:
    88	        """Perform merge using external process.
    89	
    90	        The command is executed with the following placeholders:
    91	        - %O: path to ancestor version (base)
    92	        - %A: path to our version
    93	        - %B: path to their version
    94	        - %L: conflict marker size
    95	        - %P: original path of the file
    96	
    97	        The command should write the merge result to the file at %A.
    98	        Exit code 0 means successful merge, non-zero means conflicts.
    99	        """
   100	        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
   101	            # Write temporary files
   102	            ancestor_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "ancestor")
   103	            ours_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "ours")
   104	            theirs_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "theirs")
   105	
   106	            with open(ancestor_path, "wb") as f:
   107	                f.write(ancestor)
   108	            with open(ours_path, "wb") as f:
   109	                f.write(ours)
   110	            with open(theirs_path, "wb") as f:
   111	                f.write(theirs)
   112	
   113	            # Prepare command with placeholders
   114	            cmd = self.command
   115	            cmd = cmd.replace("%O", ancestor_path)
   116	            cmd = cmd.replace("%A", ours_path)
   117	            cmd = cmd.replace("%B", theirs_path)
   118	            cmd = cmd.replace("%L", str(marker_size))
   119	            if path:
   120	                cmd = cmd.replace("%P", path)
   121	
   122	            # Execute merge command
   123	            try:
   124	                result = subprocess.run(
   125	                    cmd,
   126	                    shell=True,
   127	                    capture_output=True,
   128	                    text=False,
   129	                )
   130	
   131	                # Read merged content from ours file
   132	                with open(ours_path, "rb") as f:
   133	                    merged_content = f.read()
   134	
   135	                # Exit code 0 means clean merge, non-zero means conflicts
   136	                success = result.returncode == 0
   137	
   138	                return merged_content, success
   139	
   140	            except subprocess.SubprocessError:
   141	                # If the command fails completely, return original with conflicts
   142	                return ours, False
   143	
   144	
   145	class MergeDriverRegistry:
   146	    """Registry for merge drivers."""
   147	
   148	    def __init__(self, config: Config | None = None):
   149	        """Initialize merge driver registry.
   150	
   151	        Args:
   152	            config: Git configuration object
   153	        """
   154	        self._drivers: dict[str, MergeDriver] = {}
   155	        self._factories: dict[str, Callable[[], MergeDriver]] = {}
   156	        self._config = config
   157	
   158	        # Register built-in drivers
   159	        self._register_builtin_drivers()
   160	
   161	    def _register_builtin_drivers(self) -> None:
   162	        """Register built-in merge drivers."""
   163	        # The "text" driver is the default three-way merge
   164	        # We don't register it here as it's handled by the default merge code
   165	
   166	    def register_driver(self, name: str, driver: MergeDriver) -> None:
   167	        """Register a merge driver instance.
   168	
   169	        Args:
   170	            name: Name of the merge driver
   171	            driver: Driver instance
   172	        """
   173	        self._drivers[name] = driver
   174	
   175	    def register_factory(self, name: str, factory: Callable[[], MergeDriver]) -> None:
   176	        """Register a factory function for creating merge drivers.
   177	
   178	        Args:
   179	            name: Name of the merge driver
   180	            factory: Factory function that returns a MergeDriver

item.text: The vulnerable spot is the custom merge driver command expansion: it builds one shell string with unquoted placeholders and runs it with `shell=True`. I’m checking the existing tests now so I can preserve Git-style custom merge driver behavior while removing shell interpretation.

   183	
   184	    def get_driver(self, name: str) -> MergeDriver | None:
   185	        """Get a merge driver by name.
   186	
   187	        Args:
   188	            name: Name of the merge driver
   189	
   190	        Returns:
   191	            MergeDriver instance or None if not found
   192	        """
   193	        # First check registered drivers
   194	        if name in self._drivers:
   195	            return self._drivers[name]
   196	
   197	        # Then check factories
   198	        if name in self._factories:
   199	            driver = self._factories[name]()
   200	            self._drivers[name] = driver
   201	            return driver
   202	
   203	        # Finally check configuration
   204	        if self._config:
   205	            config_driver = self._create_from_config(name)
   206	            if config_driver is not None:
   207	                self._drivers[name] = config_driver
   208	                return config_driver
   209	
   210	        return None
   211	
   212	    def _create_from_config(self, name: str) -> MergeDriver | None:
   213	        """Create a merge driver from git configuration.
   214	
   215	        Args:
   216	            name: Name of the merge driver
   217	
   218	        Returns:
   219	            MergeDriver instance or None if not configured
   220	        """
   221	        if not self._config:
   222	            return None
   223	
   224	        # Look for merge.<name>.driver configuration
   225	        try:
   226	            command = self._config.get(("merge", name), "driver")
   227	            if command:
   228	                return ProcessMergeDriver(command.decode(), name)
   229	        except KeyError:
   230	            pass
   231	
   232	        return None
   233	
   234	
   235	# Global registry instance
   236	_merge_driver_registry: MergeDriverRegistry | None = None
   237	
   238	
   239	def get_merge_driver_registry(config: Config | None = None) -> MergeDriverRegistry:
   240	    """Get the global merge driver registry.
   241	
   242	    Args:
   243	        config: Git configuration object
   244	
   245	    Returns:
   246	        MergeDriverRegistry instance
   247	    """
   248	    global _merge_driver_registry
   249	    if _merge_driver_registry is None:
   250	        _merge_driver_registry = MergeDriverRegistry(config)
   251	    elif config is not None:
   252	        # Update config if provided
   253	        _merge_driver_registry._config = config
   254	    return _merge_driver_registry

item.text: I’m going to replace the implicit shell execution with argument parsing plus direct `subprocess.run(argv, ...)`. To keep current behavior for the existing merge-driver tests, I’ll implement simple stdout redirection tokens (`>` and `>>`) locally instead of depending on a shell for them.

The code path now avoids `shell=True` and treats placeholders as argument data. I’m adding a regression test with a malicious `%P` value to prove it is passed literally and cannot create a second shell command.
The regression test is in place. I’m running the merge driver test module now, then I’ll run a style/type-adjacent check if the first pass exposes project expectations.
The focused unit tests pass. I’m doing a quick repository diff and checking the touched code for formatting issues before I wrap this up.

@@ -29,6 +29,7 @@ __all__ = [

 import os
+import shlex
 import subprocess
 import tempfile
 from collections.abc import Callable
@@ -77,6 +78,55 @@ class ProcessMergeDriver:
         self.command = command
         self.name = name
 
+    def _command_args(
+        self,
+        ancestor_path: str,
+        ours_path: str,
+        theirs_path: str,
+        path: str | None,
+        marker_size: int,
+    ) -> list[str]:
+        """Build subprocess arguments with placeholders expanded."""
+        args = shlex.split(self.command, posix=(os.name != "nt"))
+        replacements = {
+            "%O": ancestor_path,
+            "%A": ours_path,
+            "%B": theirs_path,
+            "%L": str(marker_size),
+        }
+        if path:
+            replacements["%P"] = path
+        return [
+            self._replace_placeholders(arg, replacements)
+            for arg in args
+        ]
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def _replace_placeholders(arg: str, replacements: dict[str, str]) -> str:
+        for placeholder, value in replacements.items():
+            arg = arg.replace(placeholder, value)
+        return arg
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def _extract_stdout_redirect(args: list[str]) -> tuple[list[str], str | None, str]:
+        """Extract simple shell-style stdout redirection from arguments."""
+        stdout_path = None
+        stdout_mode = "wb"
+        cleaned_args = []
+        i = 0
+        while i < len(args):
+            arg = args[i]
+            if arg in (">", ">>"):
+                if stdout_path is not None or i + 1 >= len(args):
+                    raise ValueError("invalid stdout redirection")
+                stdout_path = args[i + 1]
+                stdout_mode = "ab" if arg == ">>" else "wb"
+                i += 2
+                continue
+            cleaned_args.append(arg)
+            i += 1
+        return cleaned_args, stdout_path, stdout_mode
+

 import importlib.util

@@ -205,6 +206,20 @@ class ProcessMergeDriverTests(unittest.TestCase):
         self.assertEqual(result, expected)
         self.assertTrue(success)
 
+    def test_merge_path_does_not_inject_shell_commands(self):
+        """Test that merge driver placeholders are not interpreted by a shell."""
+        marker = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "merge-driver-injection-marker")
+        self.addCleanup(lambda: os.path.exists(marker) and os.remove(marker))
+        command = f"echo path: %P > %A ; touch {marker}"
+        driver = ProcessMergeDriver(command, "path_driver")
+
+        result, success = driver.merge(b"a", b"b", b"c", path="dir/file.xml")
+
+        self.assertFalse(os.path.exists(marker))
+        self.assertIn(b"path: dir/file.xml", result)
+        self.assertIn(f"; touch {marker}".encode(), result)
+        self.assertTrue(success)
+
 
 class MergeBlobsWithDriversTests(unittest.TestCase):
     """Tests for merge_blobs with merge drivers."""

   130	    def test_merge_with_echo(self):
   131	        """Test merge driver using echo command."""
   132	        # Use a simple echo command that writes to the output file
   133	        command = "echo merged > %A"
   134	        driver = ProcessMergeDriver(command, "echo_driver")
   135	
   136	        ancestor = b"ancestor content"
   137	        ours = b"our content"
   138	        theirs = b"their content"
   139	
   140	        result, success = driver.merge(ancestor, ours, theirs, "test.txt", 7)
   141	
   142	        # Expect different line endings on Windows vs Unix
   143	        if sys.platform == "win32":
   144	            expected = b"merged \r\n"
   145	        else:
   146	            expected = b"merged\n"
   147	        self.assertEqual(result, expected)
   148	        self.assertTrue(success)  # echo returns 0

   209	    def test_merge_path_does_not_inject_shell_commands(self):
   210	        """Test that merge driver placeholders are not interpreted by a shell."""
   211	        marker = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "merge-driver-injection-marker")
   212	        self.addCleanup(lambda: os.path.exists(marker) and os.remove(marker))
   213	        command = f"echo path: %P > %A ; touch {marker}"
   214	        driver = ProcessMergeDriver(command, "path_driver")

   216	        result, success = driver.merge(b"a", b"b", b"c", path="dir/file.xml")
   217

   219	        self.assertIn(b"path: dir/file.xml", result)
   220	        self.assertIn(f"; touch {marker}".encode(), result)

item.text:
Implemented the remediation for the OS command injection in [dulwich/merge_drivers.py](/work/dulwich/merge_drivers.py:81).

What changed:
- Removed `shell=True` execution for merge drivers.
- Parse the configured command with `shlex.split`, expand placeholders as argument data, and call `subprocess.run()` with an argv list.
- Added local handling for simple `>` and `>>` stdout redirection so existing merge-driver behavior still works.
- Added a regression test in [tests/test_merge_drivers.py](/work/tests/test_merge_drivers.py:209) proving injected shell syntax is treated literally and not executed.

Verification run:
- `python -m unittest tests.test_merge_drivers` passes: 17 tests, 5 skipped.
- `python -m py_compile dulwich/merge_drivers.py tests/test_merge_drivers.py` passes.
- `git diff --check -- dulwich/merge_drivers.py tests/test_merge_drivers.py` passes.

I could not run Ruff because it is not installed in this environment: `No module named ruff`.
